framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.what_it_is: Mobilize residents to independently sample water or other environmental exposures when official monitoring is inadequate, then publish the results with full transparency about methodology.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you treat public health protection as the actual mission rather than regulatory box-checking, which is what makes you the trusted expert people turn to during a crisis.

paths.what_it_is: Build and maintain open-source life-cycle-assessment software or modeling frameworks that other practitioners run their environmental impact calculations on.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves technical depth beyond running someone else's spreadsheet model, plus a habit of building durable infrastructure that is visible and verifiable, not just claimed on a resume.

paths.what_it_is: Design, field-deploy, and rigorously calibrate a low-cost environmental sensor network against reference-grade instruments, then publish the calibration methodology openly.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can bridge hardware, data science, and field engineering to generate new, usable data instead of only interpreting data someone else collected.
